ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_assessment: Good quality, text-based PDF. All fields legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_assessment: Good quality, text-based PDF. Contains two separate invoices: SEC9001241 (pages 1-2, 7 chassis) and SEC9001242 (pages 3-4, 1 chassis). All fields legible.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The remarks section contains ACID, Egyptian Importer Tax ID, and Foreign Exporter ID references, but none of these constitute an LC reference. No fields labeled 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', or 'Documentary Credit' are present.

locations_found: Invoice SEC9001241, page 1: 'VOLVO BUS CORPORATION' as issuer header
Invoice SEC9001241, page 1 footer: 'Volvo Bus Corporation, SE-405 08 Göteborg, Sweden'
Invoice SEC9001241, page 2 footer: 'Volvo Bus Corporation, SE-405 08 Göteborg, Sweden'
Invoice SEC9001242, page 3: 'VOLVO BUS CORPORATION' as issuer header
Invoice SEC9001242, page 3 footer: 'Volvo Bus Corporation, SE-405 08 Göteborg, Sweden'
Invoice SEC9001242, page 4 footer: 'Volvo Bus Corporation, SE-405 08 Göteborg, Sweden'

criteria_met: Criterion 1: Certificate references invoices SEC9001242 and SEC9001241 — both invoice numbers found in invoice file.
Criterion 2: Certificate consignor 'Volvo Bus Corporation' identified as issuer of both invoices in the invoice file.
Criterion 3: Certificate consignee 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles MCV' identified in both invoices' Consignee field.
pairing_validation.motivation: All three pairing criteria are met. Certificate references two specific invoice numbers (SEC9001241 and SEC9001242), both of which are present in the invoice file. The consignor and consignee are identified in both invoices. Pairing is valid.

document_pairing.pairing_method: Certificate explicitly references both invoice numbers SEC9001242 and SEC9001241 in box 5 (Remarks) and box 6 (goods description). Both invoices found in the invoice file. Both issued by Volvo Bus Corporation with matching VAT number SE556197382601.

candidates.normalized_text: km 24 cairo ismailia desert road, el-obour city, cairo, egypt

Invoice SEC9001241, page 1, field 'Country of origin: SE'

Invoice SEC9001241, page 1, 'Additional information: Gross: 74221 Net: 62816'

candidates.source: Invoice SEC9001241, page 2 and Invoice SEC9001242, page 3

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.0 – Koncernstruktur: Search for consignor name in invoice
comparison_steps.invoice_value: VOLVO BUS CORPORATION (issuer header on both invoices); 'Volvo Bus Corporation' in footer on all pages
comparison_steps.motivation: Certifikatets consignor 'Volvo Bus Corporation' is explicitly identified as the issuer of both invoices SEC9001241 and SEC9001242. The name appears in the header as 'VOLVO BUS CORPORATION' and in the footer as 'Volvo Bus Corporation' on all invoice pages. After normalization of case, names are identical.

comparison_steps.invoice_value: Sweden (footer: 'SE-405 08 Göteborg Sweden'); VAT prefix 'SE' in SE556197382601; Country of origin: SE
comparison_steps.motivation: Certificate states consignor country as Sweden. Invoice footer explicitly states 'Sweden'. VAT number prefix 'SE' confirms Sweden per rule 4.1.2.1. Country verified.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found explicitly in invoice as issuer
Land verification via indirect identifiers – VAT prefix SE confirms Sweden

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'Volvo Bus Corporation, Bussgatan 1, SE-50464, Borås, Sweden'. Invoice issuer is 'VOLVO BUS CORPORATION' with VAT SE556197382601 and footer 'Volvo Bus Corporation, SE-405 08 Göteborg, Sweden'. After case normalization, company names are identical. The address differs (Bussgatan 1, Borås vs SE-405 08 Göteborg) but per rule 4.1.5 address differences do not cause MISMATCH — company name and country are decisive. Country Sweden is confirmed via footer and VAT prefix. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Address difference (Borås vs Göteborg) could indicate different entities
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.1.5, differences in street address or postal code do not cause MISMATCH. Company name and country are the determining factors.

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0.2 – Identify consignee in invoice using priority order
comparison_steps.invoice_value: Consignee: Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles (both invoices SEC9001241 and SEC9001242)
comparison_steps.motivation: Both invoices contain an explicit 'Consignee' field. Invoice SEC9001241 consignee: 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles, KM 24, Cairo-Ismailia Road, 00000 CAIRO, Egypt'. Invoice SEC9001242 consignee: 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles, KM 24, Cairo-Ismailia Road, 00000 CAIRO, Egypt'. Certificate consignee: 'MANUFACTURING COMMERCIAL VEHICLES M MCV'. The certificate name appears to be truncated due to layout — 'MANUFACTURING COMMERCIAL VEHICLES M' with 'MCV' on the next line. 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles' from the invoice matches the beginning of the certificate text. The 'M' at the end of line 1 appears to be the beginning of 'MCV' which is the abbreviation on line 2. Alternatively, the full name may be 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles MCV' where MCV is the abbreviation/brand name. The invoice consignee field 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles' matches the core identity. The trailing 'M MCV' in the certificate is reasonably a layout-caused truncation/abbreviation.

The certificate consignee field shows 'MANUFACTURING COMMERCIAL VEHICLES M' on line 1 and 'MCV' on line 2. The invoice consignee is 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles'. The invoice's text constitutes the complete core company name. The 'M MCV' addition in the certificate appears to be an abbreviation of the company name or a layout artifact. Per rule 4.1.3.5 (utelämnade generiska organisationsord) and looking at common naming patterns, 'MCV' is the abbreviation for 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles'. The 'M' on line 1 appears to be the start of 'MCV' broken across lines. The core name 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles' is exactly identical after case normalization. The invoice additionally shows the same address (KM 24, Cairo-Ismailia Road, Egypt) confirming same party.
Certificate consignee country is Egypt. Invoice consignee address explicitly states Egypt. Countries match exactly.

rules_applied.description: Priority order – Consignee field in invoice is used as authoritative
MATCH – company name identified and country matches

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'MANUFACTURING COMMERCIAL VEHICLES M MCV, Km 24 Cairo Ismailia Desert Road, El-Obour City, Cairo, Egypt'. Invoice consignee (both invoices): 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles, KM 24, Cairo-Ismailia Road, 00000 CAIRO, Egypt'. The core company name 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles' is exactly present in both documents after case normalization. The certificate adds 'M MCV' which is the company's abbreviation (MCV = Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles). The 'M' at end of line 1 appears to be layout truncation of 'MCV'. The address contains the same key elements: KM 24, Cairo Ismailia Road, Egypt. Minor address variations (Desert Road vs Cairo-Ismailia Road, El-Obour City vs 00000 CAIRO) are permitted per rule 4.2.4 as address differences do not cause MISMATCH when company name and country match. Country Egypt matches exactly. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The 'M MCV' suffix in certificate might indicate a different entity than 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles'
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: MCV is clearly the abbreviation of Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles. The core name is identical. The suffix is an abbreviation/brand name addition, not a different entity. Address and country confirm same party.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The Buyer field (Volvo Bussar AB, Sweden) should be used instead of Consignee field
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.2.0.2, when the invoice contains an explicit Consignee field, this is the relevant field for comparison. The Buyer field represents the purchasing entity, not the consignee.

comparison_steps.certificate_value: Volvo CKD Chassis (B0E) BERLA79, See attached invoice SEC9001242 and SEC9001241 dated 20260219
comparison_steps.invoice_value: Invoice SEC9001241: CHASSIS(B0E)BERLA79 (7 rows); Invoice SEC9001242: CHASSIS(B0E)BERL79 (1 row). Invoice dates: 2026-02-19.
comparison_steps.motivation: Certificate contains explicit invoice reference ('See attached invoice SEC9001242 and SEC9001241 dated 20260219') with both invoice numbers and date. Invoice numbers SEC9001241 and SEC9001242 match exactly. Invoice dates 2026-02-19 match certificate date 20260219 (=2026-02-19) after date format normalization. The certificate description 'Volvo CKD Chassis (B0E) BERLA79' is consistent with invoice material 'CHASSIS(B0E)BERLA79' on invoice SEC9001241 and 'CHASSIS(B0E)BERL79' on invoice SEC9001242. Per rule 4.3.5, when a generell varubeskrivning with invoice reference is provided and the references match, the goods description is verified. The invoice content (CKD chassis) is consistent with the certificate description.

The identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning from the certificate is 'Chassis (B0E) BERLA79'. On invoice SEC9001241, the material is 'CHASSIS(B0E)BERLA79' — after case normalization, 'chassis(b0e)berla79' matches. 'Volvo CKD' is a prefix/qualifier (brand + CKD assembly type) which is a non-identity-bearing addition per rule 4.3.4. On invoice SEC9001242, the material is 'CHASSIS(B0E)BERL79' — this differs from BERLA79 by missing the letter 'A'. However, since rule 4.3.5 applies and the invoice reference is verified, this minor variation in the second invoice does not prevent MATCH. The primary verification is via invoice reference.

rules_applied.description: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens – certificate references invoices SEC9001241 and SEC9001242 with date 20260219, all verified
Identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning – Chassis (B0E) BERLA79 identified in invoices
Non-identity-bearing additions – 'Volvo CKD' prefix does not affect product identity

goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate goods: 'Volvo CKD Chassis (B0E) BERLA79' with explicit reference 'See attached invoice SEC9001242 and SEC9001241 dated 20260219'. Both invoices are present in the file. SEC9001241 dated 2026-02-19 matches. SEC9001242 dated 2026-02-19 matches. Invoice SEC9001241 contains 7 rows of CHASSIS(B0E)BERLA79 — exact match with certificate's BERLA79. Invoice SEC9001242 contains 1 row of CHASSIS(B0E)BERL79 — the 'A' is missing from BERLA79, but this appears to be a variant code (BERL79 vs BERLA79). Since rule 4.3.5 applies and the generell varubeskrivning with invoice reference is the primary verification method, and the invoice content (CKD chassis) is fully consistent with the certificate description, the goods description is verified. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: BERL79 on invoice SEC9001242 vs BERLA79 in certificate could indicate different product
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Rule 4.3.5 is the primary verification method here. The invoice reference is verified, the date matches, and the invoice content (CKD chassis) is consistent with the certificate. The minor difference BERL79 vs BERLA79 on the second invoice does not override the successful invoice reference verification. Additionally, both are clearly CKD bus chassis from the same manufacturer.

comparison_steps.invoice_value: Invoice SEC9001241: Total weight:[KG] 74221, Gross: 74221; Invoice SEC9001242: Total weight:[KG] 10604, Gross: 10604
comparison_steps.motivation: Box 7 contains '84825 KG GW'. This is the verifieringsgrundande kvantitet. The value 84825 does not appear directly in either individual invoice. SEC9001241 shows Gross: 74221 and SEC9001242 shows Gross: 10604. Need to check if summation across invoices yields 84825.

comparison_steps.invoice_value: SEC9001241 Gross: 74221 KG + SEC9001242 Gross: 10604 KG = 84825 KG
comparison_steps.motivation: Applying Exception 3A. Conditions check: (4.4.5.2.1) Certificate explicitly states total quantity 84825 KG GW. Both invoices referenced and present. (4.4.5.2.2) Certificate states GW (Gross Weight) and unit KG. Invoice SEC9001241 Gross: 74221 (from Additional information) and Total weight:[KG]: 74221. Invoice SEC9001242 Gross: 10604 (from Additional information) and Total weight:[KG]: 10604. (4.4.5.2.3) Both in KG. (4.4.5.2.4) Sum: 74221 + 10604 = 84825. Certificate value: 84825. Difference: 0. Within tolerance ±0.1%. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 value (84825 KG GW) is the verifieringsgrundande kvantitet
Weight category GW explicitly stated, unit KG explicitly stated
Exception 3A – Summation of gross weights from two invoices: 74221 + 10604 = 84825 KG GW. Exact match.

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: '84825 KG GW'. This states gross weight of 84825 kilograms. Invoice SEC9001241 page 1: 'Total weight:[KG]: 74221' and 'Additional information: Gross: 74221 Net: 62816'. Invoice SEC9001242 page 3: 'Total weight:[KG]: 10604' and 'Additional information: Gross: 10604 Net: 8974'. Per Exception 3A (summation of multiple invoices), since the certificate references both invoices and states total GW: Gross weights sum = 74221 + 10604 = 84825 KG. Certificate states 84825 KG GW. Exact match (difference = 0, within ±0.1% tolerance). All conditions for 3A are met: certificate states explicit total quantity with unit and category, both invoices present, both have total gross weight lines, both in KG, and sum matches exactly. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Net weights should also be verified: 62816 + 8974 = 71790 NW
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Certificate only states GW in box 7. Per rule 4.4.2.1, box 7 is the sole verifieringsgrundande kvantitet. No NW is claimed in box 7. Net weights in invoices are additional information not contradicting the GW claim.

comparison_steps.invoice_value: Country of origin: SE (both invoices SEC9001241 and SEC9001242)
comparison_steps.motivation: Certificate box 3 states 'SWEDEN'. Both invoices contain an explicit 'Country of origin: SE' field. SE is the ISO alpha-2 code for Sweden. Per rule 4.5.4, normalization of full country name vs ISO code is permitted. SWEDEN = SE. Additionally, invoice SEC9001241 page 2 states 'Foreign Exporter Country: Sweden' and 'Foreign Exporter Country Code: SE', and declares 'these products are of EC/EEA preferential origin'. All consistent with Swedish origin.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via explicit 'Country of origin' field in both invoices
Normalization – SWEDEN normalized to match ISO code SE
MATCH – origin Sweden/SE explicitly confirmed in both invoices

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'SWEDEN'. Invoice SEC9001241 has 'Country of origin: SE'. Invoice SEC9001242 has 'Country of origin: SE'. SE is the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for Sweden. Per normalization rule 4.5.4, 'SWEDEN' and 'SE' are equivalent. Additionally, both invoices' page 2 text confirms 'Foreign Exporter Country: Sweden' and 'these products are of EC/EEA preferential origin', which is consistent with Swedish/EU origin. Origin is unambiguously verified. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in certificate.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Document pairing – multiple documents in PDF
rule_evaluation_log.motivation: Certificate file contains one certificate (ORIGINAL). Invoice file contains two invoices: SEC9001241 (pages 1-2) and SEC9001242 (pages 3-4). Certificate references both invoices. Both used for verification.
All three criteria met: invoice numbers match, consignor identified, consignee identified.
Volvo Bus Corporation found as issuer of both invoices.
Sweden confirmed via footer and VAT prefix.
Both invoices have explicit Consignee field. This field used as authoritative.
Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles identified in both invoices' consignee fields.
Certificate references invoices SEC9001242 and SEC9001241 dated 20260219. Both found, dates match, content consistent.
Gross weights: 74221 + 10604 = 84825 KG. Certificate states 84825 KG GW. Exact match.
SWEDEN = SE (ISO alpha-2 code). Both invoices confirm Country of origin: SE.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter stämmer överens. Avsändaren Volvo Bus Corporation identifieras som utställare av båda fakturorna. Mottagaren Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles återfinns i båda fakturornas mottagarfält. Varubeskrivningen verifieras via fakturahänvisning till SEC9001241 och SEC9001242 med matchande datum. Den totala bruttovikten 84 825 KG bekräftas genom summering av de två fakturornas bruttovikter (74 221 + 10 604). Ursprungslandet Sverige bekräftas av båda fakturornas fält 'Country of origin: SE'. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
warnings: Invoice SEC9001242 material 'CHASSIS(B0E)BERL79' differs slightly from certificate 'BERLA79' (missing letter 'A'), but verification is primarily via invoice reference per rule 4.3.5.
Certificate consignee shows 'M MCV' suffix not present in invoice consignee field — assessed as abbreviation/layout artifact, core name matches.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Volvo Bus Corporation, Bussgatan 1, SE-50464, Borås, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: VOLVO BUS CORPORATION, VAT No. SE556197382601, Volvo Bus Corporation SE-405 08 Göteborg Sweden (footer)
sections.rule_applied: Consignor identified as issuer of both invoices. Company name matches exactly after case normalization. Country Sweden confirmed via footer address and VAT prefix SE.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Volvo Bus Corporation' är identisk med fakturans utställare 'VOLVO BUS CORPORATION' efter normalisering av versaler. Land Sverige bekräftas via fakturans footer och VAT-prefix 'SE'.

sections.certificate_value: MANUFACTURING COMMERCIAL VEHICLES M MCV, Km 24 Cairo Ismailia Desert Road, El-Obour City, Cairo, Egypt
sections.invoice_value: Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles, KM 24, Cairo-Ismailia Road, 00000 CAIRO, Egypt (Consignee field, both invoices)
sections.rule_applied: Consignee name 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles' identified in explicit Consignee field of both invoices. Certificate's 'M MCV' suffix is the company abbreviation. Core name matches. Country Egypt matches.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'Manufacturing Commercial Vehicles' återfinns exakt i båda fakturornas mottagarfält. Tillägget 'M MCV' i certifikatet är företagets förkortning. Land Egypten matchar.

sections.certificate_value: Volvo CKD Chassis (B0E) BERLA79, See attached invoice SEC9001242 and SEC9001241 dated 20260219
sections.rule_applied: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens. Certificate explicitly references both invoices with correct numbers and date. Invoice content (CKD chassis) is consistent with certificate description.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet hänvisar uttryckligen till fakturanummer SEC9001242 och SEC9001241 daterade 20260219. Båda fakturorna finns bifogade med matchande nummer och datum. Fakturornas innehåll (CKD-chassin) överensstämmer med certifikatets varubeskrivning.

sections.invoice_value: SEC9001241: Gross 74221 KG; SEC9001242: Gross 10604 KG; Sum: 84825 KG
sections.rule_applied: Exception 3A – Summation of gross weights from two invoices. 74221 + 10604 = 84825 KG. Exact match with certificate's 84825 KG GW.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 84 825 KG bruttovikt. Faktura SEC9001241 anger brutto 74 221 KG och faktura SEC9001242 anger brutto 10 604 KG. Summan 74 221 + 10 604 = 84 825 KG, vilket exakt matchar certifikatets uppgift.

sections.rule_applied: Origin normalization – SWEDEN equals ISO alpha-2 code SE. Both invoices contain explicit Country of origin field with value SE.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger ursprungsland SWEDEN. Båda fakturorna anger 'Country of origin: SE' i dedikerat fält. SE är ISO-koden för Sverige. Ursprungsland verifierat.

output_quality_checks.notes: All 5 checkpoints are MATCH. comparison_result=IDENTICAL is consistent. All confidence values are ≥0.85. No blocking issues identified.
